danger_signals.visual_form: Nazi guards and soldiers rendered as 2D sprites in military uniforms with rifles
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; player must engage or evade

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated dog sprites, fast-moving enemies in castle corridors
Health percentage display diminishing visually on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increasing; death imminent at 0%

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique, larger enemy sprites at end of levels (distinct from standard guards)
Wall barriers blocking passage without key
danger_signals.meaning: Progression gated; hazard or enemies likely beyond

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapons and ammo sprites placed in rooms and on corpses

safety_signals.meaning: Additional life reserves; death penalty mitigation

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct architectural feature marking level completion
Certain wall tiles appear subtly different (activation tiles)
safety_signals.meaning: Hidden treasures and supplies; optional safety advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nazi insignia on uniforms, castle architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard guards in grey/brown military uniforms; officers; mutant/supernatural enemies in later episodes; boss characters in unique armor/appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle bunker aesthetic; grid-based corridor structure; Nazi-themed level designs; architectural brutalism

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through player agency (American/Polish spy backdrop)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player-controlled character implied but not prominently visually distinct in first-person view
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Weapons and equipment; strategic positioning within enemy territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animated Nazi guard sprites moving through corridors, firing weapons with visible sprite animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively pursue player, fire projectiles, react to damage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty corridors; no active sprite movement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No enemy engagement; player can freely navigate and collect resources

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gradual traversal through themed bunker rooms; increasing difficulty signaled by enemy density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter at end of level signals climax; completion screen shows stats (kill ratio, treasure ratio, speed)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Locked doors blocking passage; dense guard presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must find key, solve spatial puzzle, or overcome enemy gauntlet

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level theming shifts per episode title (Escape, Operation, Assassination); final boss represents ultimate Nazi threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating enemy types; climactic boss fight with unique visual/behavioral signature

environmental_storytelling.example: Castle bunker architecture communicates Nazi military compound; grid-based corridor layout suggests fortified underground facility
Guard density increases toward level exit; boss encounter at end signals narrative climax per episode
Limited ammunition and health pickups suggest supply constraints; player must manage resources strategically across levels
Secret areas reward exploration; treasure caches suggest looting Nazi assets as play progression mechanic
Titles ('Escape from Castle Wolfenstein', 'Die, Führer, Die!') frame narrative progression from escape to assassination

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Animated walking sprites moving through corridors in set patterns
Sprite animation changes; enemy fires projectiles at player; pursues or takes cover
Fast sprite animation; charges player; delivers melee attacks
Larger sprite with unique animation set; sustained attack pattern; high damage output; distinct death animation
Full movement speed; access to all weapons; normal HUD display
Health percentage visibly reduced on HUD; vulnerability increases; potential death upon zero health

notes: Wolfenstein 3D uses a 2.5D visual approach: 3D grid-based level geometry with 2D sprite enemies viewed from fixed angles. Danger/safety communication relies heavily on sprite animation, enemy proximity, health display, and architectural design. The game's Nazi-setting establishes clear political faction identity through uniform design, castle thematics, and episode narrative framing (escape→operation→assassination). World-state progression is linear per level; branching comes from optional secret areas. The shareware/episode structure creates narrative pacing where boss encounters mark episode climaxes.
